lineup_source: Confirmed unchanged starting XI (4-3-3) for Norway's 2026 World Cup Group I matches vs Iraq and Senegal under Ståle Solbakken

players.name: Ørjan Nyland
players.note: Born in Volda, Norway; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: Julian Ryerson
players.note: His father was born in Brooklyn, USA, and his mother in Norway; he held dual Norwegian-American citizenship before rescinding his US passport.

players.name: Kristoffer Ajer
players.note: Born in Rælingen to parents Jan Tore and Randi Ajer, both of Norwegian descent.

players.name: Torbjørn Heggem
players.note: Born and raised in Trondheim to a sporting Norwegian family; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: David Møller Wolfe
players.note: Born and raised in Bergen; parents' background is not publicly documented, typical Norwegian name.

players.name: Martin Ødegaard
players.note: Born in Drammen; son of former footballer Hans Erik Ødegaard and Lene Cecilie Ødegaard, both Norwegian.

players.name: Sander Berge
players.note: Born in Bærum to Norwegian father Åsmund Berge and Swedish mother Linnea Åbolin, both former international basketball players.

players.name: Fredrik Aursnes
players.note: Born and raised near Hareid, Norway; no documented foreign heritage.

players.name: Antonio Nusa
players.note: Born in Langhus to a Nigerian father, Joe Nusa (from Edo State), and Norwegian mother Kristin Hedelund Nordby.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.note: Born in Leeds, England, while father Alf-Inge Haaland played for Leeds United; both parents are Norwegian, family moved to Bryne, Norway, when he was three.

players.name: Alexander Sørloth
players.note: Born in Trondheim to former Rosenborg striker Gøran Sørloth and Hildegunn Roten, both Norwegian.
